name: Edwin Gray Motor
description: The Edwin Gray Motor, also known as the Gray Engine or EMA (Electro Magnetic Association) engine, is a purported over-unity device invented by Edwin V. Gray in the 1970s. Proponents claim it was capable of producing significantly more energy than it consumed, effectively violating the laws of thermodynamics. Gray asserted his engine utilized a unique method of "splitting the positive" to tap into a previously unknown energy source. He demonstrated prototypes and even sold franchises, promising revolutionary energy technology. However, the technology faced significant skepticism and scrutiny. Mainstream science views the Gray engine as a likely case of misinterpretation, fraud, or flawed experimental design. No independent verification of its over-unity claims has ever been published in peer-reviewed scientific journals. Gray faced legal troubles, including accusations of fraud and misrepresentation. The lack of reproducible results and the proprietary nature of the technology contributed to its eventual decline and discrediting in the scientific community. Despite this, the Gray engine remains a topic of interest within alternative energy and free energy circles, often cited as an example of suppressed or misunderstood technology.
subtitle: Purported over-unity free energy device.

claims.claim: The Gray engine produces more energy than it consumes.
claims.evidence: Demonstrations by Edwin Gray and testimonials from early investors.
claims.criticism: No independent, peer-reviewed verification exists. Claims of over-unity violate established physics principles, particularly the laws of thermodynamics.

claims.claim: The engine utilizes a unique method of 'splitting the positive' to tap into a new energy source.
claims.evidence: Gray's own descriptions and diagrams of the engine's operation.
claims.criticism: The term 'splitting the positive' has no scientific basis. The claimed mechanisms of energy production are not supported by established physics.

claims.claim: The technology was suppressed by powerful interests (e.g., oil companies) to protect existing energy markets.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal accounts and circumstantial evidence suggesting Gray faced obstacles in developing and marketing his engine.
claims.criticism: Lack of concrete evidence to support a deliberate suppression campaign. Legal troubles were primarily related to fraud allegations.

keyFigures.name: Edwin V. Gray
keyFigures.role: Inventor and promoter of the Gray engine

keyFigures.name: Ronald Rockwell
keyFigures.role: Investigator and critic of Gray's claims

timeline.event: Edwin Gray begins demonstrating and promoting his engine.
Gray faces legal challenges and accusations of fraud.
Interest in the Gray engine declines following Gray's death and continued lack of independent verification.

sources.author: Various
sources.work: Articles and forum discussions on free energy websites
sources.title: Various
